mapping.a_element: star product in non-commutative field theories (κ-Minkowski space-time)
mapping.b_element: formal power series representation of Lie algebra generators in the Weyl algebra

mapping.a_element: coproduct in κ-Poincaré algebra (non-symmetric treatment of momenta)
mapping.b_element: coderivations and Hopf algebra structures in the third proof method

mapping.a_element: structure constants and symmetry properties in interaction vertices
mapping.b_element: structure constants and combinatorial formulas in tensor calculations

mapping.a_element: planar and non-planar Feynman loop-diagrams in non-commutative field theories
mapping.b_element: formal right-invariant vector fields in formal group scheme theory (geometric proof)

brief_justification: While both papers involve Lie-algebraic structures and formal algebraic constructions (e.g., coproducts, Hopf algebras, and combinatorial formulas), Paper A focuses on the physical symmetry properties of interaction vertices in non-commutative field theories, whereas Paper B derives a universal formula for representing Lie algebra generators as power series in the Weyl algebra. The core mechanisms differ: Paper A's star product and coproduct address field-theoretic symmetries, while Paper B's methods are purely algebraic and combinatorial, targeting generator representations.
